title: UCR/UEA Time Series Classification Archives

data_urls.access_notes: Use official archive downloads and dataset metadata.

instructions: Choose one dataset with clear scientific or sensor semantics, not only a generic benchmark name.
Load official train/test files and inspect whether subject, device, or trial metadata are available.
If metadata are absent, restrict claims to shape-support or temporal-pattern support rather than biological or deployment mechanisms.

recommended_axes.name: dtw_shape_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Dynamic time warping or shapelet-feature similarity to training time series.

recommended_axes.name: frequency_content_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity in train-fitted spectral, autocorrelation, or frequency-domain features.

recommended_axes.name: subject_or_device_support
recommended_axes.definition: Subject, device, trial, or acquisition-condition support when metadata exist.

useful_for: Lightweight SPECTRA examples for time-series models.
Testing whether time-series classifiers generalize across shape, frequency, subject, or device novelty.
Developing approximate pairwise similarity strategies for long sequences.
leakage_risks: Many archive datasets lack the metadata needed for mechanistic deployment claims.
Duplicate or near-duplicate series can cross official splits.
Shape similarity alone is a proxy unless connected to subject, device, or scientific context.
